observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the –

a. City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) to properly plan schedule for the timely implementation of all PPAs; and

b. CEO to supervise and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under 20% DF so that necessary and timely intervention can be made to ensure their completion as planned;

c. CGSO and the OIC-City Accountant to impose the liquidated damages based on the provisions provided by the 2016 revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and

d. BAC and BAC-TWG to include in their post-qualification a comparative evaluation showing the number of projects with the same key personnel assigned by the same calculated bidder for different projects with simultaneous or overlapping successive implementation period within the City Proper.
observations.managementAction:
The CPDO and CEO coordinated the schedule of PPAS with the end-user to ensure the timely implementation, ensuring that projects are meticulously planned and ready for procurement and implementation.

BAC has mandated the BAC-TWG to conduct a comparative evaluation of key personnel for lowest winning bidders assigned to different projects awarded to the same winning bidders across bidding processes.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II. Observation No. 8 with modification.

observations.managementAction:
The CDRRMO has committed in complying in the submission requirements.

The CAIAO, in close coordination with CGSO has made diligent efforts to follow and strictly comply with the provisions of the COA Circular.

Cash donation CY reports were submitted.
observations.reason: As of the report date, the unutilized portion of cash donations was in the planning stage for future use.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the –

a. City Licensing, Permit & Franchising Office (CLPFO), City Treasurer’s Office (CTO), Management Information Services Office (MISO), and City Accounting and Internal Audit Office to perform a review on all transactions relating to business taxes which led to the erroneous calculated tax due resulting to under collection totaling ₱703,389.39 and submit the result in writing to the Audit Team for further evaluation and audit decision, without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Charge, if warranted by evidence; and

b. CLPFO, CTO, and MISO to conduct a system review on the eBLPS to identify the necessary enhancements vis-à-vis the Revised Revenue Code of the City of Balanga, Bataan of 2019 to properly identify business classification and accurately assess and collect business taxes based on the existing Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction:
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 7 with modification (for the system review and related matters).

The audit recommendation for review of transactions was implemented and results were submitted to the Audit Team for evaluation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 7 with modification.

observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended that Management develop and implement a standardized
methodology for measuring impairment allowances to ensure consistent application
of recognizing allowances for impairment in receivables.

2.6 We recommended that the Treasurer’s Office regularly review and increase
the fidelity bonds of the SDOs based on the amount of cash advance released for a
given project or activity prior to the granting of cash advance.

3.9 We recommended that the:
a. City Assessment, Appraisal and Disposal Committee (1) strengthen the
role of the Appraisal Committee by establishing procedures of assessing
unserviceable properties at year-end; (2) based on the result of assessment,
consider the disposal of said properties;
b. Appraisal Committee submit the result of assessment to CAIAO; and
c. CAIAO recognize and disclose information on impairment loss at
reporting date.

4.9 We recommended that the –
a. HRMO provide measurement on the earned leave credits using Section 3.1 of
DBM-BC No. 2016-2 dated March 29, 2016, and forward the same to
Accounting Office;
b. CAIAO (1) include the accumulated leave credits in their closing of books (2)
accrue the earned leaves using appropriate accounts and (3) disclose
information related to employee leave benefits; and
c. Budget Office (1) plan the full funding of the leave credits and (2) realign the
OPB- Vacation and Sick Leave to TLB for consistency of reports.

5.8 To prevent similar errors, we recommended that the:
a. City Budget Office assign expenses to appropriate objects based on LBC No.
156 dated April 15, 2024 and any related issuances thereafter; and
b. CAIAO establish control mechanisms that will verify the applicable account
names and codes in classifying payments of services to workers.

6.9 We recommended that the–
a. CAIAO issue directives or memoranda to ensure that information on the list
of pending court cases is forwarded to their office; and
b. CLO to submit annually a list of pending court cases, including the probability
of outcome for each case.

7.9 We recommended that:
a. The CLPFO include a verification process by assigning a designated
reviewer to validate tax rate changes before they are implemented; and
b. The CTO collect the remaining under-collection of ₱182,968.02 from the
previous year and ₱404,722.24 in 2024.

We recommended that Management instruct the Inventory Committee, in coordination with CGSO as the Secretariat, to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies, prepare, and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, in compliance with the set guidelines.

Moreover, we also recommended that the Local Chief Executive to consider hiring or assigning additional personnel to offices with inadequate manpower so that the designated and selected members of the Inventory Committee could prioritize the mandatory actual physical inventory count.
observations.managementAction:
Physical inventory count for inventories were conducted periodically and RPCI were submitted within the set guidelines.

The CGSO and the Inventory Committee have committed to diligently follow and comply in ensuring the timely and periodic conduct of inventory and submission of reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management–

a.) direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on the Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) on all funds, whether acquired through purchase or donation, including those constructed by administration and found at station on the succeeding years to establish the correct balances reported in the financial statement at year end; and

b.) establish controls in monitoring and ensure the maintenance of government vehicles' records in improving the timely and complete registration and availment of insurance under LTO and GSIS, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
The CAIAO had coordinated with the Inventory Committee to conduct physical counts across all funds to ensure accurate balances are reflected in the year-end financial statement.

The CGSO will monitor and ensure maintenance of government vehicle records for timely registration renewal and insurance update.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the –

a. Bids and Awards Committee – (1) through its BAC Secretariat, to perform/undertake the proper procedures in the pre-procurement process; and (2) to strictly ensure compliance with the requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, especially with regard to the conduct of detailed engineering; and

b. City Legal Office to ensure the compliance with Section 39.1 of the revised IRR and make the necessary actions in case the winning bidder failed to furnish the Performance Security within the required timeframe; and

c. City Engineering Office to monitor the compliance with the contract duration to ensure the timely and full implementation of the planned projects.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC requested the CEO to conduct a detailed engineering study of projects before or during programming, with all details discussed in pre-procurement to mitigate delays.

Personnel in the City Mayor's Office and City Legal Office were reminded of the guidelines for the timely submission of Performance Security and its importance in the contract signing process.

The previously noted projects have been completed. However, the CEO will monitor contract durations to ensure the timely and full implementation of future projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management -

a. instruct the Local Finance Committee and City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) to plan continuously comply with the guidelines set forth in Items 5.2 to 5.4 of DBM LBC No. 151 dated March 30, 2023; and

b. undertake adequate and proper planning of the implementation of the projects to ensure the timely and full implementation of the planned activities and to prevent the delayed implementation of projects in the future.
observations.managementAction: The CPDO has planned to implement an action designed for the proper scheduling of project implementation and ensuring timely execution of Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) proponents and end-users’ offices to diligently study the project proposals to avoid lapses in the procurement and implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented.
observations.reason: The concerned offices failed to adequately and properly plan the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the end-users’ offices to submit the appropriate project proposals for the unutilized funds and ensure their timely implementation so as to realize their intended services/purposes. Otherwise, Management should cause the reversion of the unutilized funds to the General Fund, as far as allowed by the law, for the reappropriation for basic services to the general public.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented.
observations.reason: The concerned offices failed to adequately and properly plan the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
1.8. We recommended that Management instruct the Inventory Committee, in
coordination with CGSO as the Secretariat, to conduct periodic physical inventory
count of supplies, prepare, and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories
(RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semester, respectively, in compliance with the set guidelines.

1.9. Moreover, we also recommended that the Local Chief Executive to consider
hiring or assigning additional personnel to offices with inadequate manpower so that
the designated and selected members of the Inventory Committee could prioritize the
mandatory actual physical inventory count.

2.5. We recommended that Management –

a. direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on the Property,
Plant, and Equipment (PPE) on all funds, whether acquired through purchase
or donation, including those constructed by administration and found at station
on the succeeding years to establish the correct balances reported in the
financial statement at year end; and
b. establish controls in monitoring and maintenance of government vehicles’
records in improving the timely and complete registration and availment of
insurance under LTO and GSIS, respectively.

4.6. We recommended that Management instruct the –

a. Bids and Awards Committee –
• through its BAC Secretariat, to perform/undertake the proper procedures
in the pre-procurement process; and
• to strictly ensure compliance with the requirements of R.A. No. 9184 and its
IRR, especially with regard to the conduct of detailed engineering; and
b. City Legal Office to ensure the compliance with Section 39.1 of the revised IRR
and make the necessary actions in case the winning bidder failed to furnish the
Performance Security within the required timeframe;
c. City Engineering Office to monitor the compliance with the contract duration
to ensure the timely and full implementation of the planned projects.

5.9. We recommended that Management –

a. instruct the Local Finance Committee and City Planning and Development
Office (CPDO) to continuously comply with the guidelines set forth in Items
5.2 to 5.4 of DBM LBC No. 151 dated March 30, 2023; and
b. undertake adequate and proper planning of the implementation of the projects
to ensure the timely and full implementation of the planned activities and to
prevent the delayed implementation of projects in the future.

6.10. We recommended that Management direct the –

a. City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) to properly plan the schedule
for the timely implementation of all PPAs;
b. CEO to supervise and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the
projects under the 20% DF so that necessary and timely intervention can be
made to ensure their completion as planned;
c. CGSO and the OIC-City Accountant to impose the liquidated damages based
on the provisions provided by the 2016 revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and
d. BAC and BAC-TWG to include in their post-qualification a comparative
evaluation showing the number of projects with the same key personnel
assigned by the same lowest calculated bidder for different projects with
simultaneous or overlapping implementation period within the City Proper.

7.7. We recommended that Management direct the –

a. City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (CDRRMO) to submit the
Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team on or before
the 15th day after the end of each month for verification and review;
b. City Accounting and Internal Audit Office to revisit the existing process flows
and policies to determine the necessary enhancements particularly in the area
of utilization of cash donations and to monitor compliance thereto. Moreover,
together with the City General Services Office, prepare and submit the
mandatory reports for the cash donations and donated relief goods in
compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 25,
2014; and
c. City Administrator’s Office, City Treasurer’s Office, CDRRMO, and City
Health Office to properly coordinate for the planning and utilization of the cash
donations granted to the City Government.

8.13. We recommended that Management instruct the –

a. City Licensing, Permit & Franchising Office (CLPFO), City Treasurer’s Office
(CTO), Management Information Services Office (MISO), and City Accounting
and Internal Audit Office to perform a review on all transactions relating to
business taxes which led to the erroneous calculated tax due resulting to under
collection totaling ₱703,389.39 and submit the result in writing to the Audit Team
for further evaluation and audit decision, without prejudice to the issuance of a
Notice of Charge, if warranted by evidence; and
b. CLPFO, CTO, and MISO to conduct a system review on the eBLPS to identify
the necessary enhancements vis-à-vis the Revised Revenue Code of the City of
Balanga, Bataan of 2019 to properly identify business classification and
accurately assess and collect business taxes based on the existing Revenue
Code.

9.7. With the above observations, we recommended that Management –

a. continuously comply with the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women
(PCW) –Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG)-Department of
Budget and Management (DBM)- National Economic and Development
Authority (NEDA) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated July
18, 2013 as guidance in the localization of Magna Carta of Women (MCW)
specifically in the preparation of Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and
Budget; and
b. submit to the Audit Team the action plan in addressing the delays and non-
implementation of the planned PPAs.

10.4. We commended the City for its continuous compliance with the provisions set
forth in BIR RR Nos. 2-98 and 10-2008 on the withholding and remittance of taxes
due. We recommended that the LCE, through the City Accountant, continue its
compliance with above-cited regulations.

11.5. We commended the City for its continuous compliance with the above law on
proper deductions and remittances of amount due to the GSIS in accordance with the
provisions of RA No. 8291. We recommended that the LCE, through the City
Accountant, continually comply with the above-cited provisions.

12.4. We commended the City for its continuous compliance with the above law and
recommended that the LCE, through the City Accountant sustain continuous
compliance with above-mentioned law on proper deductions and remittances of
PhilHealth contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 7875.

13.4. We commended the City for its continuous compliance with Pag-IBIG
contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679. We recommended
that the City, through the City Accountant, continually comply with the above cited
provisions.

14.3. We recommended that the LCE, upon receipt of the COA Central Office
decisions on the Petitions for Review, take the appropriate actions in accordance
therewith.

We also recommended that the City Assessor together with the City
Treasurer (a) review the list of real property owners in the City to come up with
realistic solutions on the problems causing the non-collection of previous years’
uncollected RPT taxes; and (b) coordinate with the Provincial Government
relative to the application of civil remedies for the recovery of delinquent taxes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the
CDRRMO to observe timeliness in submitting the correct forms and reports
regarding the issuance of inventory and be guided by the sections of Volume I of
Manual on NGAS for LGUs pertinent thereto.

observations.recommendation: 1.8		We recommended that the LCE instruct the a) accountable officer to submit the lacking supporting documents so as to liquidate the remaining outstanding cash advances; b) CTO to return to the BTr the remaining unutilized cash balance of the fund transfer; and c) the BAC to be more observant of the new rules, regulations and guidelines that may be issued from time to time to ensure compliance.

2.6	We recommended that the LCE instruct –

the BAC to give utmost priority on their assignment in order to complete the entire procurement process at the earliest possible time;

the BAC Secretariat, thru the BAC Chairman, to ensure the timely posting of the copies of the NOA, Contract, and NTP in the PhilGEPS website; and

the OIC-City Engineer to ensure the validity of the grounds for requests for time extension of contractors before submitting to the LCE for approval.
Henceforth, all unutilized balance of fund transfer should be reverted to the National Treasury as instructed.

3.20		We recommended that the LCE direct the CPDO, CEO and BAC and its Secretariat to:
conduct a thorough planning for the implementation of development projects by clearly identifying the project procurement timelines as laid down in the Project Procurement Management Plan and Annual Procurement Plan or their equivalent;
consider the appropriate timing and phasing of the development projects’ implementation taking into account the constraints brought about by the still on-going health crisis and set out clearly-defined roles and responsibilities in project planning, management and monitoring of the offices concerned in accordance with their mandate set forth in RA No. 7160; and
supervise and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% DF so that the necessary and timely intervention can be made to ensure their completion in a timely fashion.

4.15		We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Solid Waste Management Board -Sub Group to provide in the COVID 19 Solid Waste Management Plan the following, but not limited to, the data/information on the targeted beneficiaries, timelines/schedule of activities, movements/transport, supplies requirements and the quantification thereof so as to enable the implementers under the CENRO to efficiently and effectively deliver the services to the beneficiaries.

5.10		We reiterated our prior year recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the City’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure that (a) the gender issues reported in the GPB are supported with properly analyzed sex disaggregated data in order to provide a more complete understanding of GAD issues and concerns; and (b) the corresponding GAD objectives in every gender issue should be clearly stated to address the gender issues and concern appropriately.

6.4		We commended the City for its continuous compliance with the provisions set forth in BIR RR Nos. 2-98 and 10-2008 on the withholding and remittance of taxes due. We recommended that the LCE, through the City Accountant, continue its compliance with above-cited regulations.

7.5		We commended the City for its continuous compliance with the above law on proper deductions and remittances of amount due to the GSIS in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 8291. We recommended that the LCE, through the City Accountant, continually comply with the provisions of RA No. 8291.

8.4		We commended the City for its continuous compliance with RA No. 7875 and recommended that the LCE, through the City Accountant, sustain this adherence with above-mentioned law on proper deductions and remittances of PhilHealth contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 7875

9.4		We commended the City for its continuous compliance with the timely remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679. We recommended that the City, through the City Accountant, continually comply with the above cited provisions.
10.3		We recommended that the LCE take the appropriate actions upon the receipt of and in accordance with the decisions as issued by the concerned authorities.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the – 

(a) City Accountant, Treasurer and Assessor coordinate with each other to reconcile their records so as to present complete and verifiable RPT/SET Receivables; and

(b) City Treasurer and Assessor inform the City Accountant on a regular basis of the changes being made to the Report of Taxable and Collectible Real Properties so they can update their SLs in support of the recognized receivables to achieve check and balance as envisioned by Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the records between the City Accountant and Treasurer is coordination with the Assessor is still on-going
observations.reason: Changes being made are being identified by the Treasurer and Assessor so that they can provide necessary information to the accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the CHRMO to intensify/expedite the hiring or promotion of qualified personnel to fill-up the vacant positions so that the City could enhance its services and attain full implementation of its PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Hiring and promotion of qualified personnel is still on-going

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the City’s GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure that 
(a) the gender issues reported in the GPB are supported with properly analyzed sex disaggregated data in order to provide a more complete understanding of GAD issues and concerns; and 

(b) the corresponding GAD objectives in every gender issue should be clearly stated to address the gender issues and concern appropriately.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 5

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the purchase of additional land to accommodate the un-built and unoperated structures that are part of the City’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan;
observations.managementAction: Purchase of additional land to accommodate the un-built and unoperated structures were still on-going

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management direct the GFPS to collect and properly analyze sex-disaggregated data for integration to CBMS in order to provide a more complete understanding of GAD issues and concerns.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 12

observations.recommendation: We commended the City in its efforts to implement projects and programs to contain the spread of COVID-19 and provide basic services to its constituents. We recommended that the City, through the Local Chief Executive (LCE), continue its efforts to further assess and provide actions/solutions to the effects of the pandemic in the entire City.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:
BAC, thru its Secretariat, to ensure compliance with the posting requirements of all procurements in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and Item 9.0 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020; and
City Accountant to ensure that:
claims/disbursements are duly supported with complete documents before processing for payment to establish the validity, propriety and regularity of the claims; and
the perpetual inventory method be observed in the accounting of inventories to record supplies as assets using the inventory account and only recognize the portion of supplies inventories as expense upon consumption to reflect accurate balances of assets and expenses accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned department heads/program coordinator to (a) submit all the lacking documents enumerated above; (b) henceforth, prepare an activity budget and/or project proposal for future cash advances to set as guidelines in implementation of their planned programs; and (c) ensure that all liquidations of cash advances be supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other relevant rules and regulations to establish the validity, propriety and regularity of the expenses paid.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) concerned department heads to follow the basic controls in the receipt, recording, inventory, storage and distribution of all donations received even in the midst of a crisis and submit the reports together with the supporting documents within the required period to oversight government agencies; and (b) require the Inventory Committee to include the balances of the in-kind donations in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

We recommended that the: 

a.	City Accountant,  City Treasurer and City General Services Officer in coordination with the City Assessor 

maintain the required RPLC and PC, respectively, on all items comprising the Land account; 

provide complete necessary information on all items in the Land account to facilitate verification and reconciliation; 

fast track the reconciliation of records; and 




effect the required adjustments after the reconciliation of records is made; and


b.	Concerned personnel work on securing the land titles of the remaining 39 TCTs and appropriate funds to cover the titling cost.
observations.managementAction:
City Accounting Office has prepared RPLC, Preparation of PC by the City Treasury Office is still on going

Verification and reconciliation of necessary information for the land account is still on-going


Verification and reconciliation of necessary information for the land account is still on-going


Verification and reconciliation of necessary information for the land account is still on-going


Management has hired new personnel to help in the reconciliation process

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant review all payable accounts that have remained outstanding balances for more than two years and which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which claims were not sufficiently supported be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has already reverted some of the outstanding balances since the supplier is no longer interested in its claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and reiterated our prior year recommendation that Management instruct the implementing offices to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time for optimum attainment of the intent of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in observation No. 9

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the LDRRMC and CDRRM officers to properly plan and monitor the full implementation of the LDRRM plan towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121 to ensure disaster preparedness for the safety of the City’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office is presently coordinating with other city offices regarding the City’s PPAs. The CDRRMO also committed to ensure proper monitoring of all projects and coordination with concerned offices for the succeeding years.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the timely implementation of the social and livelihood programs so as to deliver their intended services/purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management has instructed the concerned department heads to facilitate timely implementation of the programs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GFPS collect and properly analyze sex-disaggregated data for integration to the CBMS to provide a more complete understanding of GAD issues and concerns.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS shall collect and analyze sex-disaggregated data
observations.reason: Reiterated in Obs. No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the City Treasurer’s Office, in coordination with the MISO and Assessor’s Office, reconcile their records with the Accounting Office so as to present complete and verifiable RPT/SET Receivables. Also, instruct the City Accounting Office to update its subsidiary ledgers in support of the recognized receivables to achieve check and balance on the recorded amount.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
City Accountant,  City Treasurer and City General Services Officer in coordination with the City Assessor (i) maintain the required RPLC and PC, respectively, on all items comprising the Land account; (ii) provide complete necessary information on all items in the Land account to facilitate verification and reconciliation; (iii) fast track the reconciliation of records; and (iv) effect the required adjustments after the reconciliation of records is made; and
Concerned personnel work on securing the land titles of the remaining 39 TCTs and appropriate funds to cover the titling cost.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant review all payable accounts that have remained outstanding balances for more than two years and which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which claims were not sufficiently supported be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office, in coordination with the MIS and Assessor’s Office, reconcile their records with the Accounting Office regarding the RPT and SET Receivables so as to present complete and verifiable RPT/SET Receivable.
observations.managementAction: The CTO, MISO and CAO closely collaborated and worked together for the preparation of an adjusted and verifiable Taxpayers’ List
observations.reason: Reiterated in Obs. No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Assessor, City Accountant and City Treasurer to exert all effort to identify all real property declared under the name or ownership of the City Government.  We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management be guided by PPSAS 17 in the (a) recognition of Land; (b) determination of their carrying value; and (c) impairment losses to be recognized in relation to the asset.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officers exert all efforts to account properties and their proper valuation to support the unaccounted balance
observations.reason: Reiterated in Obs. No. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exercise judiciousness in prioritizing PPAs embodied in the LDP through the preparation of structured list of PPAs to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the City of Balanga.
observations.managementAction: The CEO ensured early submission of program of works and constant monitoring of the City’s infrastructure projects under its development fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Obs. No. 6

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management be guided by DENR AO 10, s. 2006 in structuring final disposal sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction: The CENRO shall submit recommendations to the management regarding compliance with the 10-year SWMP
observations.reason: Reiterated in Obs. No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned officers to implement properly the projects as intended.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Obs. No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management be guided by PPSAS 17 in the (a) recognition of Land and Buildings; (b) determination of their carrying value; and (c) impairment losses to be recognized in relation to these assets.
observations.managementAction:
The City General Services Office explained that of the lump sum P31,833,593.66 recorded Land, Buildings and Furniture and Fixtures accounts of the City Government, P20,696,772.29 as beginning balance of land in 2000 were already coordinated with the Assessor’s Office for determination of market values and other pertinent information; P3,313,292.96 as beginning balance of buildings were already transferred to the repair and rehabilitation of the former One-Stop Shop in 2000; while P7,823,528.41 of furniture and fixtures were reported as unserviceable, exhausted, dilapidated and totally worn out and were thereby subject of a request to drop the same from the books of accounts.

Buildings worth P3,313,292.96 were already identified and taken up in the books as per PPE schedule. Furniture and fixtures in the amount of P7,823,528.41 will be dropped from the books upon SP approval, conditioned upon issuance of the required Accounting Office certification.

The City Treasurer’s Office will coordinate with the other offices for reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: The concerned offices are working together to identify the needed information for the accounts of the City Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GSO to strictly comply with the provisions of Property Insurance Law and request the GSIS to provide adequate insurance for all its insurable properties to safeguard the government from loss due to unforseen events.
observations.reason: Management is still preparing papers needed by the Government Service Insurance System to cover their properties

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the City Treasurer’s Office, in coordination with the MIS and Assessor’s Office, reconcile their records with the Accounting Office regarding the RPT and SET Receivables so as to present complete and verifiable RPT/SET Receivable. We also reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Management comply with Section 20 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume 1 through the provision by the City Treasurer Office of excel file of taxpayers to the Accounting Office which will be the basis for the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers in support of the recognized receivables. When the subsidiary ledger is created, the Accounting Office can post the collections provided by the MIS. Hence the check and balance intended by Section 20 of NGAS will be achieved.

We recommended and Management agreed that (a) the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Accountant to strictly comply with PPSAS 17 par. 14 and 71 and ensure that all PPE acquired are properly provided with depreciation; and (b) the management be guided by Sec. 122 of LGU – NGAS Manual Volume 1 which provides the general process to be followed in the control of inventory and improve the process by implementing additional controls such as, but not limited to, adding deadlines for each process, duplication of forms or any other procedure that will make the process efficient and timely recognition of PPE as prescribed by PPSAS, par. 14.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Assessor, City Accountant and City Treasurer to exert all effort to identify all real property declared under the name or ownership of the City Government.  We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management be guided by PPSAS 17 in the (a) recognition of Land; (b) determination of their carrying value; and (c) impairment losses to be recognized in relation to the asset.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Bids and Award Committee properly plan and schedule their procurement timelines and post the Notice of Awards, Contract and Notice to Proceed to the PhilGEPS Website in accordance with Section 54.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management exercise judiciousness in prioritizing PPAs embodied in the LDP through the preparation of structured list of PPAs to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the City of Balanga.

We recommended and Management agreed that the minimum standards on disaster preparedness as set by the DILG be complied. We also recommended that the management consider alternative supplier of basic commodities to ensure the availability of supplies needed in times of calamity.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management be guided by DENR AO 10, s. 2006 in structuring final disposal sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of public health and environment.
We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS collect and properly analyse sex-disaggregated data for integration to the CBMS to provide a more complete understanding of GAD issues and concerns.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the concerned officers to implement properly the projects as intended. Submit the covering MOAs for the funds transferred by source agencies. Submit also the disbursement vouchers covering the progressive or full payments for the projects.
We recommended that Management continue its compliance with above cited regulations.
We recommended that the City Government continually comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 8211

We recommended that it sustain its continuous compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittances of Philhealth contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 7875.
We recommended that Management continually comply with the above cited provisions.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MIS to fast track the billing and collection system intended for the taxable real property and collectible taxes so as to present the RPT and SET Receivables accurately.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accounting Office is now updating its subsidiary ledgers based on the listing provided by the City Treasurer’s Office. Such listing includes beginning balances covering delinquencies as well as current receivables for year 2018.

As of February 2018, the City Treasurer’s Office has already submitted the List of Taxpayers and Real Property Tax Delinquency Report to the Accounting Office.

The City Assessor’s Office has generated an updated list of taxpayers for declared real properties and submitted the same to the concerned offices last March 2017, July 2017 and May 2018.

The MISO explained that the end-user departments have completely adopted the system but because it is still new, there exists a probability of encountering bugs that may affect the reliability of outputs. Hence, the system’s need for refinement.
observations.reason:
The City Accounting Office will continue to update its records in order to fully comply with the audit recommendation.

The City Treasurer’s Office will ensure proper coordination with the Assessor’s Office and Accounting Office to update its records.

The City Assessor’s Office will ensure continuous updating of list of taxpayers as mandated. The office will also review the list of properties under restrictions.

The MISO will continue to check and  refine the system to ensure accuracy and reliability of outputs.

Reiterated in Obs. No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant review all accounts which have outstanding balances for more than two years and against which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which were not sufficiently supported be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has already reverted the amount of P36,135.14 since the supplier is no longer interested in its claims. The office has also given Sta. Ana Enterprise an opportunity to present its stale check for replacement, and has coordinated with the concerned office regarding the documents for payment to Castan Corporation.
observations.reason: The office will continuously coordinate with the suppliers and other offices for the settlement of payables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

(a) ensure the timely implementation of the social and livelihood programs so as to deliver their intended services/purposes; and
observations.managementAction:
Out of the 26 NGAs that transferred funds to the City Government, 13 were utilized while 6 were partially utilized. Of the 6 unexpended funds, 1 was subject of competitive bidding and contract was already awarded, while 1 was returned to the NGA.

The City Health Office explained that the office has accomplished the activity under the Millennium Development Goal project (ICLINIC Information System Training), and was able to purchase medicines under the PhilHealth Capitation Fund-Medicare Para Sa Masa but there were unutilized funds resulting in savings. The CHO also explained that other projects are scheduled for implementation and procurement this year.

The City Social Welfare and Development Office explained that the funding of projects implemented by the office were already utilized while the unexpended funds were returned to DBP.

The City Agriculture Office is facilitating the release of fund for one of the projects for the beneficiary.

Out of the P1.5 Million received for the Balanga City O.N.E. Project, P1,319,960.00 was allotted for lease of computers, while P190,400.00 was allotted for internet access, repair and maintenance of IT equipment, and reproduction of printed materials, all of which are still ongoing.

The City Budget Office is considering the monitoring of trust funds to counter-check the PPAs being implemented under the General Fund.

Out of the four projects implemented by the PESO, one was fully liquidated and submitted to the NGA, one projects was already awarded to the bidder and on liquidation process, while two projects were partially implemented and still subject to public bidding.
observations.reason:
There was a delay in the implementation of the projects due to failure of bidding and delay in downloading of fund  (last tranche) for the project.

The Management will ensure proper coordination and full utilization of funds to improve public service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management be guided by RA No. 10121 and Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 in planning, appropriating and implementing PPAs to provide maximum protection and minimize, if not to avoid completely, the ill effects of calamities to its constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office is presently coordinating with other city offices regarding the City’s PPAs. The CDRRMO also committed to ensure proper monitoring of all projects and coordination with concerned offices for the succeeding years.

The City Budget Office will coordinate with all sectors, committees and councils involved in the preparation of the AIP. The CBO will also inform the other offices regarding the status of the utilization of their PPAs through the Local Budget Accountability Form No. 2- Quarterly Financial Report of Operations, aside from the monthly Status of Appropriation, Allotment, Obligation and Balances for monitoring purposes.
observations.reason: The Management shall ensure proper coordination between and among concerned offices for the continuous implementation of the City’s PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the 

(a) end-users/project implementers to judiciously plan their project requirements to be submitted to the BAC for consolidation and thereafter, monitor the procurement and implementation of the projects; and
observations.managementAction:
The BAC, through its Secretariat, consolidated all the proposed procurement for 2018 based on the approved budget of every office and the Annual Investment Plan. The Annual Procurement Plan (APP) was submitted to DBM last January 2018. The BAC has suggested the early preparation and approval of AIP, annual budget and office APP to further improve the preparation of the agency APP and the procurement system. The BAC has also coordinated with the City Engineering Office for the bidding of materials for the construction of by-administration projects to address procurement concerns.

The CGSO explained that t he preparation of purchase requests (PRs) for construction materials are based on the approved Program of Works (POWs) prepared by the CEO and submitted to the CGSO. To date, the CGSO has prepared PRs of consolidated construction materials based on POWs of proposed projects for procurement.
observations.reason: The Management will ensure proper coordination between and among the concerned offices for the improvement of its planning, budgeting and procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to revisit its Solid Waste Management Facility Plan and be guided accordingly by DAO No. 10, s. 2006 in structuring its final disposal sanitary landfill to ensure protection of public health and environment.
observations.managementAction:
There was partial implementation of solid waste management projects of the City Government.

The City Engineering Office explained that a 2016 project amounting to P7-Million was completed last year which included site grading, stabilization of critical slopes and gas management. As regards leachate management, bidding for the same resulted in failure of bidding.
observations.reason:
There was failure of bidding for one of the projects.

The Management will ensure proper coordination with other concerned offices for the continuous implementation of solid waste management projects.

The CEO assured that they are reviewing and making preparations for the implementation of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to prudently monitor and evaluate the implementation of the development projects to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the City Government.
observations.managementAction:
The City Engineering Office explained that due to delay in the procurement process during the last quarter of 2017, it was deemed necessary to schedule project implementation this year.

The Management maximized the utilization of the 20% DF through re-appropriation of savings from the 2016 DF in addition to the annually funded 20% DF. However, there was failure of bidding for one of the projects amounting to P5 Million.
observations.reason:
There was delay in the preparation of documents to commence the procurement process. There was also failure of bidding for one of the projects under the 20% DF.

There was failure of bidding for one of the projects.

The Management will ensure proper coordination with other concerned offices for the continuous implementation of projects and utilization of 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS Executive Committee monitor the planned projects and activities of the GPB to ensure the effective and efficient implementation thereof and the judicious utilization of the GAD budget toward tapping potentials of men and women that may contribute to local development.
observations.managementAction:
The City Social Welfare and Development Office assured the Management that it has completed  the required staff complement. To date, the office has implemented a number of its programs as part of the GAD Plan for CY 2018. The GAD Council has initially commented that it has already conducted its meeting last February 2018 and has commenced gathering of information regarding the sectors in Balanga City last March 2018. The GAD Council has also considered attending or facilitating trainings to further prepare and equip the Secretariat to ensure proper monitoring of programs, projects and activities.

As per inter-departmental GAD Monitoring, the CSWDO has been conducting regular meeting and regular follow-up on GAD PPAs to ensure proper implementation and monitoring of all gender and development programs of the City.

The City Health Office explained that there were unexpended funds due to failure of bidding of some items during the procurement of drugs and medicines, while funding for medical and laboratory supplies was already included in another contract of the City Government. Anent the City’s health promotion projects, the CHO explained that the survey was delayed but is scheduled to undergo public bidding this year, while other health promotion activities were conducted during the City’s regular barangay learning week program and no expenses were incurred.

As regards the farmers’ field school project the City Agriculture Office commented that the fund was not fully utilized because the other partners and stakeholders also had funding for the activity.

Regarding the entrepreneurial and livelihood projects, the City Public Employment Services Office stated that the City received external funding which was then utilized to implement the projects.

As regards the Bliss Water System Project, the City Engineering Office explained that the project implementation is still ongoing.
observations.reason: The Management will ensure proper coordination between and among the concerned offices for the continuous implementation of GAD projects and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the CAO provided the annual assessment roll to the CTO so as to check the completeness of the list maintained therein. Also, we recommended that Management comply with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs, Volume I on the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables through the provision by the CTO of the excel file of taxpayers to the Accounting Office which will be the basis for the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers in support of the recognized receivables for check and balance.
observations.managementAction: In compliance with the Manual of Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations, the City Assessor's Office (CAO) submitted to the City Treasurer's Office (CTO) a copy of the list of taxpayers/ assessment roll for all declared properties as of December 31, 2016 last March 10, 2017. For the ongoing General Revision of real property assessment, the CAO submitted to the CTO a copy of the list of taxpayers/ assessment roll as of June 30, 2017 last July 11, 2017
observations.reason: Reiterated on Observation No. 1, of the 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GSO to strictly comply with the provisions of Property Insurance Law and request the GSIS to provide adequate insurance for all its insurable properties to safeguard the government from loss due to unforseen events.
observations.reason: Management is still preparing papers needed by the Government Service Insurance System to cover their properties

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Accounting Office comply with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I on the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables using as basis the certified list of taxpayers submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office and maintain subsidiary ledgers detailing the accounts and taxes due thereof. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MIS to fast track the billing and collection system intended for the taxable real property and collectible taxes so as to present the RPT and SET Receivables accurately.

We recommended that the Management be guided by PPSAS 17 in the (a) recognition of Land and Buildings; (b) determination of their carrying value; and (c) impairment losses to be recognized in relation to these assets. We also recommended that Management conduct physical inventory of Furniture and Fixtures to verify the existence and actual status of each item, make a report thereon and decide on their disposition, if needed.

We recommended that the City Accountant review all recorded payables to ensure that only valid claims are recognized in the books. Accounts which have outstanding balances for more than two years and against which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which were not sufficiently supported may be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that Management (a) ensure the timely implementation of the social and livelihood programs so as to deliver their intended services/purposes; and (b) request from source agencies authority to use unutilized funds, otherwise, return the unutilized balances as required under Paragraph 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that Management be guided by RA No. 10121 and Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 in planning, appropriating and implementing PPAs to provide maximum protection and minimize, if not to avoid completely, the ill effects of calamities to its constituents.

We recommended that the Management instruct the (a) end-users/project implementers to judiciously plan their project requirements to be submitted to the BAC for consolidation and thereafter, monitor the procurement and implementation of the projects; and (b) BAC and consolidate all PPMPs into an APP for proper scheduling of procurement pursuant to Section 10 of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to revisit its Solid Waste Management Facility Plan and be guided accordingly by DAO No. 10, s. 2006 in structuring its final disposal sanitary landfill to ensure protection of public health and environment.

We recommended and Management agreed to prudently monitor and evaluate the implementation of the development projects to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the City Government.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS Executive Committee monitor the planned projects and activities of the GPB to ensure the effective and efficient implementation thereof and the judicious utilization of the GAD budget toward tapping potentials of men and women that may contribute to local development.

We commended the City Government for its efforts in continuously remaining at the top in good governance. We recommended that Management continue following its strategy map in building a family-oriented city with a strong learning atmosphere to ensure sustainable development and participatory governance and attain its vision of becoming a “University Town in 2020-The Emerging Hub for Tech Businesses”.

We recommended that the City Agriculturist continuously revisit the project and its beneficiaries to ensure that the objectives thereof are fully achieved and henceforth, proper evaluation of beneficiaries should be conducted to ensure that projects are properly implemented and targeted benefits are derived therefrom.

We commended the City Government for its continuous compliance with the provisions set forth in Revenue Regulations Nos. 2-98 and 10-2008 on the withholding and remittance of taxes due. We recommended that Management continue its compliance with above cited regulations.

We commended the City Government for its continuous compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittances of GSIS premiums in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 8291. We recommended that the City Government continually comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 8211

We commended the City Government for its continuous compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittances of Pag-IBIG contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679. We recommended that Management continually comply with the above cited provisions.

We recommend that the GSO and the Accounting Office trace and gather relevant information as required by Sections 13 and 15 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II to account and reconcile the differences in the amount recorded for PPE Accounts.
observations.managementAction: The CAIAO and CGSO worked together to reconcile the differences, and have determined that the actual difference is only ₱31,833,593.66 which represent the beginning balances of Land, Buildings and Furnitures & Fixtures. To date, the CGSO has already accounted ₱3,313,292.96 for Buildings account, which pertain to the repair and rehabilitation of the CIty Government's one-stop shop. The CGSO has also determined that the amount of ₱7,823,528.41 and ₱20,696,772.29 pertain to properties acquired more than 14 years ago, which include furniture and fixtures, among others.
observations.reason: Reiterated on Observation No. 2, of the 2017 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant set-up RPT/SET Receivable based on the amount due and collectible for the year together with the certified list of taxpayers furnished by the City Treasurer, and prepare and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The City MISO is currently working on linking the eRPT System to the Treasurer’s Office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Chief Executive ensure that all Land owned are registered in the name of the City Government. (i) Instruct the City Assessor to update the record of all lots owned by the City Government; (ii) City General Service Office to gather, complete and keep on file all necessary documents relating to the ownership of land by the City; (iii) City Accountant to prepare and maintain RPLC and trace the composition of the recorded beginning balance of the Land account of P20,696,772.29 and; (iv) GSO and the City Accountant to reconcile the recorded amount of PPE with the physical count and trace the composition of PPE accounts with the beginning balances.
observations.managementAction: The management is currently securing clearances from Land Bank of the Philippines and Department of Agrarian Reform to continue processing of transfer of city properties. Updating of list of lots declared under the name of the Municipality/City of Balanga is on-going.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2, of the 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Ensure that all lessees are bounded by a contract of lease; and Amend and make provisions on the Contract of Lease that will clearly specify the mode of payment, penalties and legal actions to be imposed concerning violations of the provisions.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the recommendations given by the COA. Strict implementation of policies stipulated in the contract resulted in a ₱290,647.30 amount of delinquencies as of September 30, 2016.
observations.reason: Renewal of contracts under study/review of the new Local Chief Executive

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAO provide the annual assessment roll to the CTO so as to check the completeness of the list maintained therein. Also, we recommended that the Management comply with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I on the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables through the provision by the CTO of the excel file of taxpayers to the Accounting Office which will be the basis for the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers in support of the recognized receivables for check and balance.

We recommended that the GSO and the Accounting Office trace and gather relevant information as required by Sections 13 and 15 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II to account and reconcile the differences in the amount recorded for PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GSO to strictly comply with the provisions of the Property Insurance Law and request the GSIS to provide adequate insurance for all its insurable properties to safeguard the government from loss due to unforeseen events.

We recommended that the LDRRMC monitor the full implementation of the LDRRM plan towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121 to ensure the City’s disaster preparedness for the safety of its constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) strengthen the GAD Focal Point System to be able to conduct gender audit and institutionalize GAD database/sex-disaggregated data which shall be the bases of GAD Plan and Budget; and (b) ensure utilization of the GAD funds towards tapping potentials of men and women that may contribute to local development.

We recommended that the Local Development Council judiciously plan, monitor, evaluate and prioritize the implementation of projects to maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund on programs/projects/activities outlined under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and achieve the desired benefits in socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) City Licensing Franchising and Permit Office and City Public Safety Office to coordinate with the Land Transportation Office for the proper implementation of the provision of Section 82(f) of City Ordinance No. 14 s. 2014 and Section III(D) of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007-01; and (b) Sangguniang Panglusod to revisit the ordinance and revise the same to include sanctions that may be applied for violations thereof.
We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the maximum limit of Pag-IBIG employer’s contribution to ₱100.00 per month per official/employee.
